Motion For Permission To Conduct Early Discovery Including Serving A Deposition Subpoena To Erica J. Sandberg, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Alex Lemberg, Esq., Law Office of, (415) 921-9315, for Plaintiff.
John Roach, Esq., Law Office of, (415) 851-4557, for and with Non-party Erica J. Sandberg.

RE: PLAINTIFF R. MCCOY's Motion For Permission To Conduct Early Discovery Including Serving A Deposition Subpoena To Erica J. Sandberg.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem Judge. The pro tem Judge and both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf. Non-Party appears via Zoom at the request of the pro tem Judge to provide testimony. The pro tem Judge administers witness oath to Non-Party, via Zoom. Non-party responds affirmatively.

The court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

The matter is argued. Upon conclusion of oral argument, Plaintiff's Motion is denied.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem Judge at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com.
